summary:
The initial click upon mouth opening results from transient discoordination where the condyle moves ahead relative to its normal position over the articular disc before re-aligning smoothly. 

This summary captures both core aspects—transient displacement causing the audible release—and focuses specifically on how such movements relate clinically within temporomandibular joint function.
